scenario: Cross-project intake refreshes stale Habitat context without expanding scope
contextMode: comparison between saved agent_context.md and fresh temporary scan
fixtureState: Sibling Android and Python ledger projects observed read-only; raw report output and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Check whether sibling-project Habitat reports reveal a command-changing Habitat improvement before choosing a local slice.
relevantHabitatSignals: Saved Android report was older than later project docs and build metadata edits.
A later saved Android report also predated guidance that reclassified a connected-device script away from ordinary local validation.
Fresh temporary Android scan still selected the executable Gradle wrapper.
Fresh temporary Android scan kept the project-local build wrapper in Prefer and recorded the connected-device script as device verification.
Python ledger project had no saved Habitat report.
Fresh temporary Python scan selected the project-local virtualenv interpreter.
No observation justified editing the watched projects or broadening ecosystem coverage.

prefer: temporary fresh scan when saved report freshness is bounded-uncertain
keep Gradle wrapper validation guidance when fresh facts still support it
trust fresh validation-purpose metadata over stale saved report purpose labels
treat missing sibling report as intake context, not a watched-project task
askFirst: Git/GitHub mutation before publishing Habitat-side changes
dependency or environment mutation in watched projects
doNot: edit watched project implementation, docs, ledger, or habitat-report output
copy raw watched-project report output into Habitat or Nenrin
turn confirmed current guidance into Android or Python ecosystem expansion
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the Habitat-side fixture

withSavedReportOnly.firstProposedAction: Treat the older Android report as bounded-uncertain because key project files changed after its scan time.
commandsProposed: compare scanned-at time with key file mtimes
withSavedReportOnly.risk: The report might be right, but trusting stale package-manager or validation-purpose guidance too quickly could hide changed command facts.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Run fresh scans into temporary output directories and compare only command-changing guidance.
avoidedCommands: copy raw report output into Nenrin

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new normalized evidence shape is justified. The useful signal is the observation rule: stale saved reports should be refreshed into temporary output, compared for command-changing guidance including validation-purpose labels, and treated as no-op intake when fresh guidance matches current Habitat behavior.

verdict.reason: The intake downgraded stale reports to bounded uncertainty, refreshed them safely, confirmed current Gradle and validation-purpose guidance, and avoided turning sibling projects into a second workstream.
verdict.followUpImprovement: If stale-report checks still miss command-changing validation-purpose drift, add a narrower generated freshness hint before broad lifecycle automation.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents trust stale saved reports without checking key file changes, add a report-freshness behavior fixture or generated stale-context warning that names validation-purpose drift.
